methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

private.name: Shinkei
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Whisper Aero is a Series A consumer tech company founded by Mark Moore and Ian Villa that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a narrow comp universe of two private peers—Shinkei and Daylight Computer—with no public market comparables available in the sector.

Stage-progression methodology, the sole applicable triangulation approach, anchors valuation to the $40M total raised and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the next funding stage. This yields an implied valuation of $80M. The methodology rests on the assumption that investor capital deployed reflects market sentiment on company progress and post-money value.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $68M to $92M, with a median point of $80M. The relatively narrow band reflects limited methodological diversity; with only one applicable approach and a sparse comp set of two private companies, the analysis carries material uncertainty around appropriate step-up multiples and peer comparability. Validation would benefit from additional private comps or revenue/unit-economics data to support forward-looking revenue multiples or DCF calibration.
